title: Rajiv Gandhi Zoological Park
tagline: Rajiv Gandhi Zoological Park — wildlife reserve of Maharashtra

overview.short: The Rajiv Gandhi Zoological Park, commonly known as the Rajiv Gandhi Zoo or Katraj Zoo, is located in Katraj, Pune district, Maharashtra State, India.
overview.description: The Rajiv Gandhi Zoological Park, commonly known as the Rajiv Gandhi Zoo or Katraj Zoo, is located in Katraj, Pune district, Maharashtra State, India. It is managed by the Pune Municipal Corporation. The 130-acre (53 ha) zoo is divided into three parts: an animal orphanage, a snake park and a zoo and includes the 42-acre (17 ha) Katraj Lake.

topPlaces.name: Katraj
topPlaces.description: Katraj is suburb of Pune in the Indian state of Maharashtra, and within the jurisdiction of Pune Municipal Corporation. Katraj is famous for its Peshwa-era (18th century) lake that supplied water to the city during that period.

topPlaces.name: Bharati Vidyapeeth
topPlaces.description: Bharati Vidyapeeth is a private deemed to be university established in Pune, India. Bharati Vidyapeeth has campuses across the country at New Delhi, Navi Mumbai, Sangli, Pune, Solapur, Kolhapur, Karad, Satara, and Panchgani.

topPlaces.name: Dhankawadi
topPlaces.description: Dhankawadi was an outlying borough of the city of Pune in the state of Maharashtra. It lies on the southern edge of the city along old Route 4 just north of where the Route 4 bypass leaves old Route 4, about 6.5 kilometres (4.0 mi) south of the Pune city Centre.

topPlaces.name: Union Biblical Seminary
topPlaces.description: Union Biblical Seminary (UBS) is a theological seminary founded by Wesleyan and Methodist denominations in Pune, India. UBS started as a Marathi-medium Bible School opened by the Free Methodist Church and was finally established in 1953 and initially located in Yavatmal, before relocating to Pune in 1983.

topPlaces.name: Vadgaon Budruk
topPlaces.description: Vadgaon Budruk, known as Vadgaon in the local area, is one of the many upcoming suburbs of Pune, Maharashtra, India. It is located on Sinhgad Road.

topPlaces.name: Blades of Glory Cricket Museum
topPlaces.description: Blades of Glory Cricket Museum is a cricket memorabilia museum located in Pune, Maharashtra, India. It was founded by Rohan Pate, a former Maharashtra cricketer and was inaugurated by Sachin Tendulkar in 2012.

topPlaces.name: Taljai Hills
topPlaces.description: Taljai is a hill located in the heart of Pune, India that has been designated as a wildlife reserve. The road to this hill passes through the Shivaji Maratha College campus with a picturesque mountain path, with sharp turns.

topPlaces.name: Swargate bus station
topPlaces.description: Swargate bus station consists of two adjacent bus stations in Pune, operated by the Maharashtra State Road Transport Corporation (MSRTC) and the Pune Mahanagar Parivahan Mahamandal Limited (PMPML). It is located in the locality of Swargate in Pune, opposite the Swargate Police Station.

items.note: Katraj is suburb of Pune in the Indian state of Maharashtra, and within the jurisdiction of Pune Municipal Corporation. Katraj is famous for its Peshwa-era (18th century) lake that supplied water to the city during that period.

Bharati Vidyapeeth is a private deemed to be university established in Pune, India. Bharati Vidyapeeth has campuses across the country at New Delhi, Navi Mumbai, Sangli, Pune, Solapur, Kolhapur, Karad, Satara, and Panchgani.

Dhankawadi was an outlying borough of the city of Pune in the state of Maharashtra. It lies on the southern edge of the city along old Route 4 just north of where the Route 4 bypass leaves old Route 4, about 6.5 kilometres (4.0 mi) south of the Pune city Centre.

Union Biblical Seminary (UBS) is a theological seminary founded by Wesleyan and Methodist denominations in Pune, India. UBS started as a Marathi-medium Bible School opened by the Free Methodist Church and was finally established in 1953 and initially located in Yavatmal, before relocating to Pune in 1983.
Vadgaon Budruk, known as Vadgaon in the local area, is one of the many upcoming suburbs of Pune, Maharashtra, India. It is located on Sinhgad Road.

Blades of Glory Cricket Museum is a cricket memorabilia museum located in Pune, Maharashtra, India. It was founded by Rohan Pate, a former Maharashtra cricketer and was inaugurated by Sachin Tendulkar in 2012.

Taljai is a hill located in the heart of Pune, India that has been designated as a wildlife reserve. The road to this hill passes through the Shivaji Maratha College campus with a picturesque mountain path, with sharp turns.

Swargate bus station consists of two adjacent bus stations in Pune, operated by the Maharashtra State Road Transport Corporation (MSRTC) and the Pune Mahanagar Parivahan Mahamandal Limited (PMPML). It is located in the locality of Swargate in Pune, opposite the Swargate Police Station.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Rajiv Gandhi Zoological Park?
faq.a: The best time to visit Rajiv Gandhi Zoological Park is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Rajiv Gandhi Zoological Park?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Pune Airport (16 km) and the nearest railway station is Pune Junction (9 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Rajiv Gandhi Zoological Park?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Rajiv Gandhi Zoological Park at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Rajiv Gandhi Zoological Park?
faq.a: Stays in Rajiv Gandhi Zoological Park start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Rajiv Gandhi Zoological Park?
faq.a: The top attractions are Katraj, Bharati Vidyapeeth, Dhankawadi, Union Biblical Seminary, Vadgaon Budruk.
